question: How do I create a new product listing on Amazon?
ground_truth: Go to Inventory > Add a Product in Seller Central. Search for the product or create a new listing. Fill in required fields including title brand description and images. Set price quantity and fulfillment method then submit for review.

question: What is the Amazon FBA fulfillment fee for a small standard item?
ground_truth: FBA fulfillment fees for small standard items start at $3.22 per unit and vary based on product size and weight.

question: What is the monthly storage fee for FBA inventory?
ground_truth: Monthly storage fees are $0.87 per cubic foot from January through September and $2.40 per cubic foot from October through December.

question: What is the Order Defect Rate threshold I must stay below?
ground_truth: The Order Defect Rate must stay below 1% to maintain good account health.

question: How often does Amazon disburse payments to sellers?
ground_truth: Amazon disburses payments every 14 days to the seller's bank account.

question: What is the cancellation rate threshold for seller-fulfilled orders?
ground_truth: The cancellation rate must stay below 2.5% for seller-fulfilled orders.

question: What is required to enroll in Amazon Brand Registry?
ground_truth: You need an active registered trademark that matches the brand name on your products and packaging.

question: What are the benefits of Amazon Brand Registry?
ground_truth: Benefits include proactive brand protection A+ Content Brand Analytics Sponsored Brands advertising and Amazon Stores.

question: What is the late shipment rate threshold?
ground_truth: The late shipment rate must stay below 4%.

question: How do I handle a customer return as an FBM seller?
ground_truth: You must match or exceed Amazon's 30-day return policy. Process returns through Orders > Manage Returns and issue refunds once items are received.

question: What is the Inventory Performance Index target?
ground_truth: The target IPI score is 450 or above.

question: What happens to inventory stored longer than 365 days?
ground_truth: Long-term storage fees are applied to items stored over 365 days in Amazon fulfillment centers.

question: What is the minimum disbursement amount from Amazon?
ground_truth: The minimum disbursement is $1. Balances below this carry over to the next settlement period.

question: What are referral fees on Amazon?
ground_truth: Referral fees are category-based percentages of the sale price typically ranging from 8% to 15%.

question: How many images are recommended for a product listing?
ground_truth: A minimum of 1 image is required but 7 or more images are recommended for optimal performance.

question: What is the valid tracking rate requirement?
ground_truth: The valid tracking rate must be above 95%.

question: How do I fix a suppressed listing?
ground_truth: Go to Inventory > Fix Stranded Inventory or Manage All Inventory filter by suppressed status click the issue to see the reason and edit the listing to fix it.

question: What is A+ Content and who can use it?
ground_truth: A+ Content is enhanced product descriptions with images and text modules available to Brand Registry members. It can increase conversion by 3-10%.

question: What are the image requirements for Amazon product listings?
ground_truth: Images must have a white background and be a minimum of 1000 pixels on the longest side.

question: What is FBA?
ground_truth: FBA stands for Fulfillment by Amazon. Sellers send inventory to Amazon warehouses and Amazon handles picking packing shipping and customer service.

question: What is Seller Fulfilled Prime?
ground_truth: Seller Fulfilled Prime allows qualified sellers to offer Prime shipping while fulfilling orders themselves. Requirements include same-day handling and on-time delivery above 93.5%.

question: How do I add backend keywords to my listing?
ground_truth: Add search terms in the backend of your Seller Central listing. Use no punctuation no repeated words and include synonyms and alternate spellings.

question: What is ACoS in Amazon advertising?
ground_truth: ACoS stands for Advertising Cost of Sale. It represents the ratio of ad spend to sales generated by those ads.

question: What are Sponsored Products ads?
ground_truth: Sponsored Products are keyword-targeted ads that promote individual product listings in Amazon search results and product pages.

question: How long does it take for a new listing to appear on Amazon?
ground_truth: New listings may take 15 minutes to several hours to appear on Amazon after submission.

question: What is the Customer Service Dissatisfaction Rate threshold?
ground_truth: The Customer Service Dissatisfaction Rate must stay below 25%.

question: How do I request early payment disbursement?
ground_truth: You can request early disbursement once per settlement period through your Seller Central account settings.

question: What is an FNSKU label?
ground_truth: An FNSKU label is an Amazon barcode used to identify your specific product in FBA. It is required unless using stickerless commingled inventory.

question: What is a Plan of Action in Amazon seller support?
ground_truth: A Plan of Action is a document submitted to Amazon in response to a policy violation. It should include the root cause corrective actions taken and preventive measures.

question: How do I set up a bank account for Amazon payments?
ground_truth: Go to Settings > Account Info > Deposit Methods in Seller Central. Add and verify your bank account details.

question: What is the Individual selling plan cost?
ground_truth: The Individual selling plan costs $0.99 per item sold with no monthly subscription fee.

question: What is the Professional selling plan cost?
ground_truth: The Professional selling plan costs $39.99 per month regardless of the number of items sold.

question: What is an A-to-z Guarantee claim?
ground_truth: An A-to-z Guarantee claim is filed by a buyer when they have an issue with an order. Unresolved claims count against your Order Defect Rate.

question: How do I improve my product ranking on Amazon?
ground_truth: Optimize your title with primary keywords add A+ Content use backend search terms run Sponsored Products campaigns and improve your conversion rate.

question: What is the Restock Inventory tool?
ground_truth: The Restock Inventory tool in Seller Central provides demand forecasting recommendations to help sellers avoid stockouts.

question: What documents do I need to register as a seller on Amazon?
ground_truth: You need a government-issued ID bank account information credit card tax identification number and a phone number to register.

question: How does Amazon handle FBA returns?
ground_truth: For FBA sellers Amazon automatically processes customer returns. Items are returned to fulfillment centers and customers are refunded automatically.

question: What is a removal order in FBA?
ground_truth: A removal order is a request to have your inventory returned to you or disposed of from an Amazon fulfillment center to avoid storage fees.

question: What is the difference between FBA and FBM?
ground_truth: FBA uses Amazon warehouses for fulfillment while FBM means the seller stores and ships orders themselves. FBA has higher fees but less operational burden.

question: How do I create an Amazon Store?
ground_truth: Amazon Stores are free multi-page brand storefronts available to Brand Registry members. Create one through the Stores builder in Advertising console.

question: What triggers listing suppression?
ground_truth: Common reasons include missing required attributes non-compliant images price violations ASIN mismatches incorrect categories or selling restricted products without approval.

question: What is the Sponsored Brands ad format?
ground_truth: Sponsored Brands ads feature your brand logo a custom headline and multiple products appearing at the top of search results.

question: How do I track my FBA inventory levels?
ground_truth: Monitor inventory levels in Inventory > Manage FBA Inventory in Seller Central. Set reorder alerts and use the IPI dashboard.

question: What is commingled inventory in FBA?
ground_truth: Commingled inventory means your products are stored with identical products from other sellers without individual FNSKU labels. Requires a barcode exemption.

question: What happens if my account health drops?
ground_truth: Poor account health can result in listing suppression selling privileges suspension or account deactivation depending on the severity and duration.

question: How do I enroll in Seller Fulfilled Prime?
ground_truth: You must meet performance requirements including same-day handling on-time delivery above 93.5% and shipping with Amazon-approved carriers.

question: What are Amazon Automatic ad campaigns?
ground_truth: Automatic campaigns target keywords automatically based on your product listing content. They are useful for discovering new converting keywords.

question: What is the Inventory Performance Index?
ground_truth: The IPI is a score measuring how efficiently you manage your FBA inventory based on excess inventory sell-through rates stranded inventory and in-stock rates.

question: How do I appeal a suspension on Amazon?
ground_truth: Submit a Plan of Action through the Performance > Account Health section. Your POA must clearly address the root cause corrective actions and prevention steps.

question: What are virtual product bundles on Amazon?
ground_truth: Virtual product bundles allow Brand Registry members to create bundle listings combining two or more products without physically bundling them. Customers purchase the bundle and each item ships separately.
